implementation_summary: A portrait-oriented 2D tower defense playable ad where the player drags and drops weapons into slots on their blue castle to auto-fire projectiles at the enemy red castle. The first castle to reach 0 health loses. Built as a single HTML file using Canvas API, with base64 encoded assets.

screen.responsive_strategy: Scale canvas to fit window while maintaining aspect ratio, centering it vertically and horizontally. Background image will be centered and cropped to fill the screen.

gameplay.objective: Destroy the enemy red castle before the player's blue castle is destroyed.
gameplay.primary_interaction: Drag and drop weapon icons from the bottom UI into empty slots on the player's castle.
gameplay.loss_or_timeout_condition: Player castle health reaches 0 or 30 seconds elapse.

entities.behavior: Static on the bottom-left/center. Has 3 empty slots for weapons. Takes damage from enemy projectiles.
Static on the top-right/center. Auto-fires projectiles at the player castle at a fixed interval. Takes damage from player projectiles.
Draggable from bottom UI. Once placed in a slot, auto-fires Projectile_1 at the enemy castle.
Draggable from bottom UI. Once placed in a slot, auto-fires Projectile_1 at the enemy castle with different stats.
Moves linearly from player weapon slot to enemy castle. On impact, reduces enemy health and triggers explosion particles.
Moves linearly from enemy castle to player castle. On impact, reduces player health and triggers explosion particles.
Static background, scaled to cover the canvas.

asset_plan.processing: Base64 encode. Scale and crop to fit 720x1280.
Base64 encode. Scale down to fit bottom half of screen.
Base64 encode. Scale down to fit top half of screen.

asset_plan.use: Deployable unit icon and placed unit visual
asset_plan.processing: Base64 encode. Used instead of .psb character files for native HTML5 compatibility.
Base64 encode as data URI. Autoplay on first interaction.
Base64 encode as data URI. Play on collision.

interaction_flow.system_response: Enemy castle begins firing projectiles at the player castle, depleting its health bar to create urgency.

interaction_flow.player_action: Touch and drag Weapon_1 from the bottom UI panel
interaction_flow.system_response: Weapon icon follows the pointer. Castle slots are highlighted.

interaction_flow.player_action: Release touch over an empty slot on the Blue Castle
interaction_flow.system_response: Weapon snaps to the slot and immediately begins auto-firing Projectile_1 at the Red Castle.
Projectiles exchange. Health bars deplete. Damage numbers pop up on impacts.
When a health bar reaches 0, the destroyed castle shakes and fades. A 'Download Now' CTA overlay appears.

parameters.name: enemy_fire_rate_ms
parameters.gameplay_effect: Determines how fast the enemy attacks, directly impacting difficulty and urgency.

parameters.name: player_fire_rate_ms
parameters.gameplay_effect: Determines how fast placed player weapons attack.

parameters.name: castle_max_health
parameters.gameplay_effect: Scales the duration of the playable ad.

parameters.name: projectile_damage
parameters.gameplay_effect: Changes how many hits are required to destroy a castle.

acceptance_criteria: Game is contained entirely within a single HTML file under 5MB.
No external network requests are made for assets or libraries.
Player can successfully drag a weapon from the UI and drop it into a castle slot.
Placed weapons automatically fire projectiles at the enemy.
Enemy castle automatically fires projectiles at the player.
Health bars accurately reflect damage taken from projectile collisions.
A clear Win or Loss state is presented when a castle's health reaches 0.
non_goals_v1: Parsing or rendering .psb (Photoshop Big) files; standard PNG weapons will be used as deployables instead.
Complex physics-based destruction of the castles (simple screen shake and particle bursts will be used).
Resource management (e.g., gold or mana costs for deploying units).
Moving castles (castles will remain static to simplify the 2D canvas logic).
